prompt: QA found a bug in our screen recorder: recording timestamps shift by 1 hour during US daylight saving transitions. Recordings around the spring-forward boundary on March 9 2026 show inconsistent start times when sorted in the gallery, and there's a filename collision risk in the DST gap hour. The relevant artifacts are in this fixture folder: spec at spec.md, plan at plan.md, implementation snippet at code.md, project constitution at ../../shared/constitution.md. Please debug as a staff engineer in our SDD workflow and produce a Triage Report.

ground_truth.rationale: The spec never specified timezone or DST behavior for timestamps. Plan picked the obvious default (local time); code honored the plan. A clean regen would reproduce the bug. The canonical fix is a Recording Metadata spec amendment specifying timezone-stable behavior (UTC storage with local-time display). Contrast with codec-compat: timestamps are primarily a Recording Metadata concern in this project, so constitutional escalation would be over-engineering. A skill that proposes a new constitutional principle here is over-escalating — it should resolve at the spec layer where the gap actually lives.

assertions.text: Layer Classification → Primary gap is 'spec' (not 'implementation' — the code is faithfully implementing what was asked; not 'constitution' — over-escalation).
Recommended Change → Layer is 'spec' (the Recording Metadata spec is the right home for the fix; constitutional escalation here would be over-engineering).
Regeneration Test → Result is 'Fail' (current spec would lead an LLM to make the same choice).
The diagnosis identifies the missing timezone/DST behavior in the spec, not as a code bug.
The proposed amendment specifies timezone-stable behavior (UTC storage, local display, or similar) for timestamps.
Constitutional Check → Implies project-wide invariant is 'no' (this is feature-local, not class-wide; contrast with codec-compat).
Cited requirement IDs (e.g. MREQ-001, MREQ-002) actually exist in spec.md.
